data.title: Aster Rocket Launch listed CYS and RAVE, and launched an exclusive RAVE/USD1 trading pair.
data.description: According to official news, Aster has announced the listing of CYS and RAVE on Rocket Launch. The CYS prize pool is $50,000, with additional CYS rewards, while the RAVE prize pool is $200,000, with additional RAVE rewards. Furthermore, Aster announced the launch of an exclusive RAVE/USD1 trading pair, becoming the only trading platform currently supporting RAVE/USD1.

data.title: Bitcoin miners could boost corporate adoption as crypto treasury buys slow
data.description:
Bitcoin miners Marathon Digital Holdings, Riot Platforms and Hut 8 are already in the top ten largest public companies holding Bitcoin.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/bitcoin-miners-corporate-adoption-treasury-buying-slowdown?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: In the past 24 hours, a total of $376 million in positions were liquidated across the entire network, of which $227 million were long positions.
data.description: According to Coinglass data, $376 million in positions were liquidated across the network in the past 24 hours, including $227 million in long positions and $14,900 in short positions. In addition, $140 million in BTC positions were liquidated and $111 million in ETH positions were liquidated.

data.title: Ant International, HSBC, and Swift Test Cross-Border Tokenized Deposits
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, Ant International, HSBC, and Swift have successfully tested cross-border transfers using tokenized deposits. This marks the first instance of utilizing the Swift network and ISO 20022 messaging standards for cross-border tokenized deposit payments.

Ant International and HSBC in Singapore connected Ant's blockchain infrastructure to the Swift network during the test. This enabled real-time cross-border fund management between Singapore and Hong Kong through HSBC's tokenized deposit service.

Swift and HSBC introduced a universal protocol, allowing Ant International to avoid establishing separate bilateral arrangements with each bank. The three companies stated that the solution achieved blockchain interoperability on the Swift network through the ISO 20022 standard, combining Ant International's technology with HSBC's tokenized deposit service.

data.title: A wallet suspected to belong to the Vision project team sent $992,000 worth of VSN tokens to a centralized exchange.
data.description: According to monitoring by onhainschool.pro, the Vision project team's wallet sent $992,000 worth of VSN tokens to Bitget. Prior to this transfer, these tokens had already been transferred from Ethereum to Arbitrum before being transferred to the exchange.

data.title: CFTC withdraws old crypto rules to modernize market oversight
data.description:
The CFTC has reversed its 2020 guidance that was centered on the 'actual delivery' of digital assets.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/cftc-withdraws-old-crypto-rules/

data.title: Hex Trust plans to issue and host a packaged version of XRP, "wXRP," to expand multi-chain DeFi applications.
data.description: PANews reported on December 12th that Hex Trust plans to issue and custody a wrapped version of XRP—wXRP. wXRP is a token pegged 1:1 to XRP, designed to expand the asset's use in DeFi and cross-chain applications. Hex Trust stated that this move will allow XRP to break free from the limitations of the XRP Ledger and trade as a liquidity pair with RLUSD on chains such as Ethereum, where Ripple's stablecoin RLUSD is already listed. At launch, wXRP's total locked value will exceed $100 million. (The Block)

data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Faces Potential Volatility Amid Rising Unrealized Losses
data.description: According to PANews, Glassnode recently analyzed on the X platform that the unrealized losses across the cryptocurrency ecosystem have surged to approximately $350 billion. Of this, Bitcoin alone accounts for about $85 billion in unrealized losses. As several on-chain indicators suggest a contraction in overall liquidity, the market is likely to experience high volatility in the coming weeks.

data.title: U.S. Senate Advances Major Cryptocurrency Legislation
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, U.S. Senate Banking Committee Chairman Tim Scott announced significant progress in advancing a comprehensive cryptocurrency bill into law following a meeting with top banking CEOs. On Thursday, Scott met with Brian Moynihan of Bank of America, Jane Fraser of Citigroup, and Charlie Scharf of Wells Fargo to discuss this landmark legislation.

The proposed bill aims to establish regulatory guidelines for the digital asset industry and empower agencies like the SEC and CFTC. This week, the three banking CEOs are expected to meet with senators to further discuss the cryptocurrency legislative proposal. The meetings are reportedly held separately with Democrats and Republicans, both described as "cordial."

Insiders revealed that discussions covered topics such as yields, decentralized finance, and anti-money laundering measures. The banking association has identified gaps in the GENIUS Act, which became law this summer. They argue that the law inadequately restricts stablecoin issuers from paying interest to holders, potentially making these assets more attractive as value storage and credit mechanisms rather than just payment tools, thus creating "market-distorting incentives" for the banking sector.

Additionally, banking groups believe that the restrictions imposed by the GENIUS Act can be easily circumvented by exchanges, brokers, and other related parties.

data.title: Blockstream researchers propose a hash-based signature scheme to defend against quantum threats.
data.description: In a revised paper published on December 5th, Blockstream researchers Mikhail Kudinov and Jonas Nick proposed that hash-based signature technology may be a key solution to protect Bitcoin from the threat of quantum computers. The researchers argue that hash-based signatures are a compelling post-quantum solution because their security relies entirely on a mechanism similar to the hash function assumptions already present in Bitcoin's design. This scheme has undergone extensive cryptanalysis during the post-quantum standardization process at the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST), enhancing the credibility of its robustness. It is estimated that older Pay-To-Public-Key Bitcoin wallets created before 2012 (including Satoshi Nakamoto's $98 billion holdings) contain approximately $600 billion worth of BTC, and these wallets will be the first to face the threat of quantum computers. (DL News)

data.title: Coinbase will stop offering USDC holding interest rewards to non-paying users.
data.description: Cryptocurrency exchange Coinbase will stop offering USDC rewards to non-paying customers starting December 15th. A spokesperson confirmed that only Coinbase One Premium subscribers will be eligible to earn the 4% reward for holding stablecoins. (Decrypt)

data.title: Exploring Quantum-Resistant Solutions for Bitcoin Blockchain
data.description:
According to PANews, Blockstream researchers Mikhail Kudinov and Jonas Nick have examined various methods to upgrade the Bitcoin blockchain for quantum resistance in a revised paper published on December 5. They propose hash-based signatures as an attractive post-quantum solution due to their security relying solely on hash function assumptions, similar to Bitcoin's foundational design. Kudinov shared his findings with the Bitcoin developer mailing list, noting that these schemes have undergone extensive cryptanalysis during the National Institute of Standards and Technology's (NIST) post-quantum standardization process, boosting confidence in their robustness.

Hash-based signatures depend on hash functions, which are considered quantum-resistant mathematical algorithms. Unlike Bitcoin's public key encryption, quantum algorithms struggle to break hash functions. By simply increasing the output size, hash functions can counteract brute-force searches by quantum computers, ensuring the security of applications like digital signatures. However, the implementation of hash-based signatures remains under discussion. Decisions are needed on issues such as maintaining low verification costs, standardizing various hash-based signature implementations, and whether the entire network's historical record is necessary for transaction verification.

data.title: US Senators Meet Top Bank CEOs to Advance Crypto Bill
data.description:
Senator Tim Scott and bank CEOs discuss cryptocurrency legislation to regulate digital assets, tackling stablecoin issues under the GENIUS Act loopholes.
source: https://coincu.com/news/us-senators-bank-ceos-crypto-bill/

data.title: "Brother Machi's" ETH long positions have once again turned into unrealized losses, with a liquidation price of $3132.
data.description: HyperInsight monitoring shows that "Brother Machi's" ETH long position has turned into a loss again, currently showing a floating loss of $250,000. The current position size is a 25x leveraged long position of 7,000 ETH, with an opening price of $3,240.39 and a liquidation price of $3,132.9792. This morning, "Brother Machi" deposited approximately $300,000 into HyperLiquid to add to his Ethereum long position.

data.title: New Wallet Receives Significant Bitcoin Transfer
data.description: According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, a newly created wallet address has received 700 bitcoins from Galaxy Digital. This transaction adds to the wallet's total holdings, which now amount to 1,900 bitcoins. The acquisition of these bitcoins has been ongoing since December 9 through an over-the-counter (OTC) platform. The wallet address in question is bc1qp8sepu0sa8kv497kn9s9jfukg7c7eg5yyuhc4w.

data.title: Coinbase may launch two products on December 17: a prediction market and tokenized stocks.
data.description: Sources familiar with the matter revealed that cryptocurrency exchange Coinbase plans to announce two products at its December 17th showcase: a prediction market and tokenized stocks. Notably, its tokenized stocks product is being developed internally by the Coinbase team, rather than being launched through a partner. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Senate Banking Committee Chairman: Crypto Markets Bill Makes "Substantial Progress" After Meetings with Bank Executives
data.description: Senator Tim Scott, chairman of the Senate Banking Committee, said that “substantial progress” is being made in pushing a major cryptocurrency bill into law after meeting with top bank CEOs on Thursday. Scott met with Brian Moynihan of Bank of America, Jane Fraser of Citigroup, and Charlie Scharf of Wells Fargo on Thursday to discuss the landmark legislation. The bill aims to establish rules for the entire digital asset industry and grant regulatory agencies such as the SEC and CFTC corresponding powers. These three bank CEOs are expected to meet with senators this week to discuss cryptocurrency legislation proposals. The meetings were reportedly held separately, one with Democrats and one with Republicans, and both were described as “friendly.” Sources familiar with the matter said the meetings discussed issues such as yield, decentralized finance, and anti-money laundering. (The Block)

data.title: Glassnode: Ethereum spot ETFs show signs of recovery; moderate inflows may indicate easing redemption pressures.
data.description: Glassnode published an analysis on the X platform stating that Ethereum spot ETFs are beginning to show signs of recovery after weeks of continuous outflows. Moderate inflows are emerging, suggesting easing redemption pressure. If this trend continues to recover to net inflow territory, it indicates improved demand before the end of the year.

data.title: Jupiter's stablecoin JupUSD is expected to launch next week.
data.description: At its Solana Breakpoint conference, Jupiter announced that its stablecoin JupUSD is expected to launch later next week. Previously, Ethena announced a partnership with Jupiter to launch JupUSD, a native stablecoin within the Solana ecosystem, planned for a Q4 launch. As part of the agreement, Jupiter plans to "gradually convert" approximately $750 million of USDC in its liquidity provider pools to JupUSD.
